content:
I couldn’t believe it—I had finally discovered the secret to living forever! I felt like a superhero with endless time to play, learn, and explore. But as I stood there celebrating, a shadow appeared. It was Death, looking not scary but kind. He handed me a small card and said, “When you realize living forever sucks, call this number. I’ve got a job offer for you.”

I looked at the card—it had a tiny phone number and a picture of a clock with no hands. I asked, “What kind of job?”

Death smiled. “Helping people finish their stories. Sometimes, living forever means missing out on new beginnings. When you’re ready to help others say goodbye, just call me.”

I put the card in my pocket, feeling a little less scared. Maybe living forever would be an adventure, but knowing Death was just a phone call away made everything feel a bit easier.
